purpose: pre-refactor LLM baseline anchor for Phase 35 context path refactoring

baseline_definition.name: LLM Baseline (Pre-Refactor)
baseline_definition.semantic_role: Phase 35 before-state anchor — the frozen reference point against which all Phase 35 context path changes will be compared
baseline_definition.note: This baseline IS NOT a single-run absolute truth. The band (S34.5.2) will define its normal fluctuation range.

baseline_is: a frozen before-state of the current LLM coaching pipeline
a regression anchor — any Phase 35 change must not degrade below this band
a structural snapshot of prompt/context path as it exists today
a companion to the rules baseline, not a replacement
baseline_is_not: a replacement for Phase 32 rules baseline (which remains the stability anchor)
a claim of optimal LLM behavior
a provider migration or model upgrade

relationship_to_rules_baseline.rules_baseline_role: stability anchor — pure rule-engine performance floor
relationship_to_rules_baseline.llm_baseline_role: pre-refactor anchor — LLM-augmented coaching snapshot before Phase 35
relationship_to_rules_baseline.comparison_note: These two baselines are side-by-side references with different purposes. Rules baseline measures the rule-engine floor. LLM baseline measures the current LLM-augmented state. They are not competitors — they anchor different questions.

phase_35_input.what_phase_35_will_change: LLM context path refactoring (stable prefix / retention layers / mainline wiring / stream parity)
phase_35_input.what_this_baseline_provides: A before-state with: (1) overall score band, (2) per-dimension band, (3) context path structural fingerprint, (4) immutable config snapshot
phase_35_input.comparison_method: Phase 35 after-state scores compared against this baseline's mean ± band. A successful refactor stays within or above the band on all key dimensions.

next_stage: S34.5.2 — baseline band confirmation (3-5 repeated runs)
